museum.dek: March 2017: Nintendo Switch launches on March 3.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
items.body: Nintendo launches Switch with a world that rewrites what open air can mean.
items.sourceNote: Switch launch references list March 3, 2017.

items.title: Nier: Automata
items.body: Yoko Taro's android tragedy becomes a cult object with mainstream reach.
items.sourceNote: Regional timing varies; March 2017 is safe.

items.title: PlayerUnknown's Battlegrounds
items.body: Battle royale stops being a mod lineage and becomes the next gravity well.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 23, 2017 early access.

items.title: Resident Evil 7: Biohazard
items.body: Capcom makes horror first-person, grimy and intimate again.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2017 calendar lists January 24.

items.title: Yakuza 0
items.body: A prequel turns karaoke, crime and side-stories into a wider western breakthrough.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2017 calendar lists January 24 for western PS4 release.

items.title: Horizon Zero Dawn
items.body: Guerrilla's robot wilderness gives PS4 a bright new exclusive world.
items.sourceNote: Regional timing varies around late February/early March.

items.title: Persona 5
items.body: Atlus's stylish school-year RPG finally reaches the West.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2017 calendar lists April 4.

items.title: Mario Kart 8 Deluxe
items.body: A Wii U rescue mission becomes an evergreen Switch system-seller.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2017 calendar lists April 28.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: Nintendo Switch launches
items.body: The hybrid console arrives with a tiny launch shelf and one era-defining Zelda.
items.sourceNote: Switch launch references list March 3, 2017.

items.title: Xbox One X launches
items.body: Microsoft sells power, 4K and premium hardware into a market already reshaped by PS4 Pro.
items.sourceNote: Microsoft E3 and launch references support November 2017 context.

items.title: Nintendo Classic Mini: SNES arrives
items.body: Mini-console nostalgia becomes another retail scarcity ritual.
items.sourceNote: 2017 release references list late September 2017; verify UK stock context before final copy.

items.title: Game Pass begins
items.body: Xbox Game Pass launches in 2017 and quietly points toward Microsoft's future identity.
items.sourceNote: 2017 Xbox service context; exact public launch dates should be verified against Microsoft sources.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Streams and servers
items.body: PUBG makes streaming feel like a spectator sport of panic, inventory management and sudden silence.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Services and stores
items.body: Switch social life is screenshots, friend codes, local multiplayer and the novelty of taking a console anywhere.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Forums and feeds
items.body: Battlefront II turns loot boxes into a mainstream argument about fairness, gambling and full-price games.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: Discord and Twitch are now ordinary gaming infrastructure, while YouTube criticism moves faster than print ever could.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like Nintendo had stepped through a door and found the lights back on. Switch made old arguments feel suddenly out of date.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: Breath of the Wild made discovery feel communal even when played alone; everyone had a different mountain, trick or disaster to report.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: PUBG made PC gaming look messy, funny and thrilling again, while loot-box anger made the business model part of ordinary player conversation.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: By Christmas, the year felt absurdly full: Zelda, Mario, Persona, Nier, Horizon, Cuphead, PUBG and a console you could play on the train.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
